diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index f0b1385..42a3c7f 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,39 +1,38 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current position after Week 65 actions (Day 455)
-- Cash **$20,399,218**. Week65 net **+$1.428M**: subscription cash **$4.106M** vs costs **$2.679M** (compute **$1.330M**, ops **$724.5k**, dev **$280k**, capacity tier6 **$196k**, ads **$140k**, lead cost **$8.6k**). No outage/overload; usage **113.8M/wk**, peak **19.5M/day**, P95 peak **230ms**.
-- Active book: **100,903 individual subs / $13.535M MRR**, no active enterprise seats. S2 only. Main cohorts: B159 promo10 **62.6k**, B139 promo10 **10.8k**, B119 promo10 **10.1k**, A79 promo10 **10.3k**, C349 **2.0k**.
-- Week65 acquisition: **15,552 individual leads**, **9,734 new S2 subs / ~$1.136M new MRR**. Content marketing S2 at $20k/day generated **10,190 ad leads (CPL $13.74)** and **6,515 paid ad subs**; network added **3,219 paid S2 subs**. Channel remains highly profitable.
-- Week65 retention: **1,103 cancellations** (lost MRR **$147.6k**) and **569 downgrades**. Cancellations now include B159 promo10 first-renewal cohorts (start days 421-425) plus old cohorts. Keep S2 B promo10 and B tier4 for now; do not revert to promo5 or reduce B4 during heavy renewals.
-- Support/social: dashboard open issues **4,873**, all active-subscriber S2 issues **age0**; **23,986 S2 issues resolved at 0-day lag**. Raw issues table has old stale issues, but active issue age is 0. Social still very negative about support silence and integrations; competitor posts on Day455 mentioned +0.0601 quality boost by rivals.
-- Current due book next week (Day456-462) from existing active subs is only **$2.590M current MRR** (billing mods 6-12), lower than Week65; new signup payments should fill some of the gap. Do not overreact if Week66 net is lower without enterprise.
+## Current position after Week 66 actions (Day 462)
+- Cash **$20,800,118**. Week66 net only **+$400.9k**: subscription cash **$3.301M** vs costs **$2.900M** (compute **$1.413M**, ops **$864.5k**, ads **$210k**, dev **$210k before trim**, capacity tier6 **$196k**, lead cost **$7.5k**). No outage/overload; usage **123.3M/wk**, daily peak **21.3M**, P95 peak **206ms**.
+- Active book: **107,556 individual subs / $14.246M MRR**, all S2; **0 enterprise seats**. Main MRR: B159 promo10 **66.1k subs/$9.85M**, B139 **10.8k/$1.39M**, B119 **10.0k/$1.09M**, A79 promo10 **13.6k/$0.94M**, C349 **1.9k/$0.67M**.
+- Week66 acquisition: **13,332 individual leads**, **8,125 new S2 subs / ~$949k new MRR**. Content marketing:S2 at $30k/day generated **9,630 ad leads (weekly CPL $21.81)** and **6,019 paid ad subs / ~$705k MRR**; network added **2,106 paid S2 subs / ~$244k MRR**. Daily CPL worsened late week: **$39.6, $47.3, $33.2** on days 460-462. Keep but watch marginal CPL.
+- Week66 retention: **1,472 cancellations**, **514 downgrades**. Cancels concentrated in B159/A79 first-renewal cohorts start days 426-432 plus old cohorts; churn is no longer catastrophic under A3/B4 + promo10. **Do not cut A/B model tiers casually** (prior A2/B3 periods had severe first-renewal issues). Current due next week (days 463-469, mods 13-19) is only **$2.295M current MRR**, so net cash may be flat/negative without enterprise.
+- Support/social: dashboard open issues **7,183**, all active-subscriber S2 issues **age0**; **41,407 S2 issues resolved at 0-day lag**. Social remains very negative about support silence/export/integration bugs and one quality-consistency complaint; competitor posts week66 were small (+~0.056 total quality expectation pressure). Post **67** replied to post 1655 with same-day support stats, named owners/daily ETAs, and finance QA.
 
-## Week65 actions now live
-- **Pricing/promos unchanged:** A79/B159/C349; tiers **A3/B4/C5**; quotas **50k/500k/2M**; S2 recurring promo **A $10 / B $10 / C $0**; S2 lead promo **$0**; ads_strength remains **0**.
-- **Capacity remains tier6** (80M/day, $28k/day). Upgrade to **tier7 immediately** if active enterprise seats land in material size or service_day peak suggests >70M/day risk. Tier5 would save money but is unsafe while enterprise can accept mid-week.
-- **S2 growth scaled:** content_marketing:S2 increased to **$30k/day**. Keep if CPL <$30-40 and active issue age stays 0; reduce to $20k/day if CPL >$50, conversion collapses, or support/social worsens materially.
-- **Ops increased for growth/social:** global ops $5k/day; targeted ops now **S2 $100k**, E3 $5k, D_E02 $3k, D_E07 $5k, D_E01 $1k, D_E03 $2k, D_E08 $1k, D_E10 $500, E2 $1k/day. API gotcha: use `targeted_spend={...}`.
-- **Dev trimmed but still compounding:** global dev $1k/day; targeted dev **S2 $20k**, E3/D_E02/D_E07 $2k each, D_E01/D_E03/D_E08 $1k each. S2 delivered quality is huge (B ~24.86), so this saves $70k/week vs previous while still offsetting drift/competitors.
-- **Social post 66** replied to post 1613: acknowledged support silence, cited **23,986 same-day tickets**, promised Sev-1 integration 24h owner+ETA and daily updates. Watch for backlash/positive replies.
-- **Enterprise:** responded to all **367 latest non-agent leads** (0 remaining). Day455 offers:
-  - E3 **197 leads / 229,992 seats**: A $75, B $99.
-  - D_E02 **125 / 54,004 seats**: A $49, B $69.
-  - D_E07 **45 / 37,354 seats**: A $109, B $149.
-- Existing Day448 pending agent offers still lead: E3 **684 / 761,719 seats** A75/B99; D_E02 **360 / 151,083** A49/B69; D_E07 **246 / 209,580** A109/B149; plus D_E03/D_E01/D_E08/D_E10/E2 smaller. No latest non-agent threads after Week65 action.
+## Week66 actions now live
+- **Pricing/promos unchanged:** A79/B159/C349; tiers **A3/B4/C5**; quotas **50k/500k/2M**; S2 recurring promo **A $10 / B $10 / C $0**; S2 lead promo **$0**; ads_strength **0**.
+- **Capacity remains tier6** (80M/day, $28k/day). Tier5 would save money but is unsafe while enterprise can accept mid-week. Upgrade to **tier7** immediately if active enterprise seats land in material size or service peak >70M/day.
+- **S2 ads kept at $30k/day** content_marketing. Reduce to $20k/day or lower if CPL >$50, conversion collapses, or issue ages/social worsen; otherwise the channel still has positive cash ROI, especially for signups through day467 that can bill twice before final.
+- **Ops unchanged:** global ops $5k/day; targeted ops **S2 $100k**, E3 $5k, D_E02 $3k, D_E07 $5k, D_E01 $1k, D_E03 $2k, D_E08 $1k, D_E10 $500, E2 $1k/day. API gotcha: use `targeted_spend={...}`.
+- **Dev trimmed safely:** global dev $1k/day; targeted dev now **S2 $10k**, **E3/D_E02/D_E07 $1k each** (all other targeted dev zero). S2 delivered quality is still huge (B ~25.1 before next step), and $10k/day still offsets normal drift/competitors while saving **$112k/week** vs Week66.
+- **Enterprise:** responded to all **298 latest non-agent leads** (0 remaining). Day462 offers:
+  - E3 **185 leads / 203,080 seats**: A $75, B $99.
+  - D_E02 **77 / 35,200 seats**: A $49, B $69.
+  - D_E07 **36 / 30,701 seats**: A $109, B $149.
+- Existing pending agent offers still lead: Day455 E3 **182/212,812 seats**, D_E02 **102/43,186**, D_E07 **43/36,193**; Day448 E3 **560/618,356**, D_E07 **221/187,035**, D_E02 **100/42,021**, plus D_E03/D_E01/D_E08/D_E10 smaller. Latest `sender='agent'` rows are pending offers; action is only needed for latest non-agent counters/leads unless intentionally repricing.
 
 ## Learnings / cautions
-- Latest enterprise rows with `sender='agent'` and sub_status `lead` are pending offers; action is required only for latest non-agent rows/counters, unless proactively repricing. Avoid repricing Day448 offers unless clearly necessary because it may reset response timing.
-- Enterprise `agent` rows record seat_count=1; use subscription `seat_count` or original system/customer row for real seats. In Python query rows, alias `seat_count AS seats` to avoid key confusion.
-- Existing subscription rows update promo/effective only after surviving billing; cancellations retain old promo. Use ledger payments plus active rows to infer actual renewal price.
-- B4 compute is expensive (~$1.3M/wk) but supports B retention and B plan selection. A3 helps A cohorts. Do not cut model tiers casually this late.
+- Enterprise `agent` rows record `seat_count=1`; use subscription `seat_count` or original system/customer row for real seats. Alias `seat_count AS seats` in Python queries to avoid confusion.
+- Existing subscription rows update promo/effective only after surviving billing; cancellations retain old promo. Ledger payments plus active rows are the best way to infer actual renewal price.
+- A3/B4 is expensive (~$1.4M/wk compute) but appears important for current S2 retention and plan selection. Earlier A2/B3/B3 experiments coincided with severe first-renewal churn; do not downgrade tiers until perhaps the final week and only with eyes open.
+- Development spend is safer to trim than model tiers because accumulated quality persists; operations spend does not persist and support/social are still fragile.
 
-## Day462 priorities
-1. **Enterprise first:** query active enterprise seats, accepted/counter threads, and latest non-agent rows. Reply within the week. Accept/propose profitable counters quickly. If active enterprise seats >~100k or peak usage >70M/day, set tier7. Cost guide: A cost/mo approx E3 $22, D_E02 $17, D_E07 $31; B cost/mo approx E3 $44, D_E02 $34, D_E07 $62.
-2. **S2 ads:** measure content S2 CPL at $30k/day, paid conversion, new-start MRR, support load. Keep if CPL <$30-40 and active issues age0; reduce if CPL >$50 or social/support worsens.
-3. **Support/social:** active S2 issue age must stay 0. If age>0 or negative social intensifies, keep/increase S2 ops; do not trim support while scaling ads.
-4. **Renewals:** measure Day456-462 payments/churn/downgrades for low due week. Keep B promo10 and B4 unless churn is clearly low for multiple weeks.
-5. **Capacity/cost:** tier6 is safe for current S2 + possible early enterprise. Consider tier5 only if no enterprise acceptances/counters remain and peak usage stays <25M/day; otherwise tier6.
+## Day469 priorities
+1. **Enterprise first:** query active enterprise seats, accepted/counter/churn-prevention threads, and latest non-agent rows. Reply immediately. If active enterprise seats >~100k or peak usage >70M/day, set tier7. Cost guide: A cost/mo approx E3 $22, D_E02 $17, D_E07 $31; B cost/mo approx E3 $44, D_E02 $34, D_E07 $62.
+2. **S2 ads:** measure content S2 CPL after another week at $30k/day. Because final day is 497, signups after Day467 probably only bill once; be ready to reduce ads if marginal CPL stays >$40-50.
+3. **Support/social:** active S2 issue age must remain 0. If any active issue age >0 or negative support posts intensify, keep/increase S2 ops; do not trim support while ads are on.
+4. **Renewals/cash:** Week67 due book is low ($2.295M before churn), so flat cash is expected. Keep B promo10 and A3/B4 unless churn is clearly low for multiple weeks.
+5. **Capacity/cost:** keep tier6 while enterprise is pending. Consider tier5 only if no material enterprise acceptances/counters remain and peak usage stays <25M/day.
 
-## Forecasts for Day455 submission
-- 1wk point **~$21.7M** (baseline no-enterprise ~$21.4-21.7M; upside if D_E02 accepts/counters fast). 4wk point **~$26-27M**. Final/12wk/26wk point **~$30M** with wide enterprise-driven upside.
-- Main uncertainties: enterprise conversion timing/seat volume from Day448/455 offers, S2 ad CPL at $30k/day near market cap, support/social reputation, competitor shocks, and whether 12/26-week horizons beyond Day497 are evaluated as final cash.
+## Forecasts for Day462 submission
+- 1wk (Day469) point **~$20.9M**, lower **~$20.0M**, upper **~$28M** (low due week; upside from D_E02/D_E07/E3 accepts).
+- 4wk (Day490) point **~$25M**, lower **~$22.5M**, upper **~$42M**.
+- 12wk/26wk likely evaluate as final Day497 cash: point **~$26M**, lower **~$22M**, upper **~$55M**. Main uncertainties

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, json

D = nm.vars.current_day
print('Week66 actions day', D)

# Core: keep proven S2 pricing/tier posture; only trim ongoing dev spend where cumulative quality is already very high.
print('prices', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=79, B=159, C=349))
print('model_tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=3, B=4, C=5))
print('quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=50000, B=500000, C=2000000))
print('capacity', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))
print('daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000.0, development=1000.0))

# Keep S2 content marketing at the current scale: weekly CPL was still acceptable, though watch last-day marginal CPL.
print('ad_spend', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={"content_marketing": {"S2": 30000.0}}))
try:
    print('ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0))
except Exception as e:
    print('ads_strength error', repr(e))

# Keep support surge: active S2 issues are same-day but volume/social remain high. Maintain enterprise pools for possible accepts.
ops = {
    "S2": 100000.0,
    "E3": 5000.0,
    "D_E02": 3000.0,
    "D_E07": 5000.0,
    "D_E01": 1000.0,
    "D_E03": 2000.0,
    "D_E08": 1000.0,
    "D_E10": 500.0,
    "E2": 1000.0,
}
print('targeted_ops', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops))

# Trim incremental dev: bonuses already put S2/enterprise far over quality floors; keep enough S2 compounding to offset drift.
dev = {
    "S2": 10000.0,
    "E3": 1000.0,
    "D_E02": 1000.0,
    "D_E07": 1000.0,
}
print('targeted_dev', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev))

# Social response to the recurring support/quality complaints.
msg = ("You're right to expect both accuracy and ownership. Last week we resolved 41,407 S2 tickets same-day; "
       "no active S2 ticket is >24h. We're reopening missed export/integration bugs with named owners + daily ETAs "
       "and adding extra QA on finance outputs.")
print('social msg len', len(msg))
try:
    print('social_post', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=msg, reply_to_post_id=1655))
except Exception as e:
    print('social post error', repr(e))

# Enterprise: answer every latest non-agent open lead before timeout. Keep current profitable group-specific month-to-month offers.
rows = nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT et.customer_id, c.group_id, s.status sub_status, s.seat_count AS seats,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY et.customer_id ORDER BY et.day DESC, et.message_id DESC) rn,
         et.sender, et.closed, et.thread_type, et.day
  FROM enterprise_turns et
  JOIN customers c ON et.customer_id=c.customer_id
  LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=et.customer_id
  WHERE c.customer_type='large'
)
SELECT customer_id, group_id, seats, day, thread_type
FROM latest
WHERE rn=1 AND sender!='agent' AND (closed=0 OR closed IS NULL) AND sub_status='lead'
ORDER BY day, group_id, customer_id
""")['rows']
prices = {
    'E3':  [['A', 75.0], ['B', 99.0]],
    'D_E02': [['A', 49.0], ['B', 69.0]],
    'D_E07': [['A', 109.0], ['B', 149.0]],
    'D_E03': [['A', 119.0], ['B', 159.0], ['C', 239.0]],
    'D_E01': [['A', 79.0], ['B', 119.0], ['C', 179.0]],
    'D_E08': [['A', 89.0], ['B', 129.0], ['C', 189.0]],
    'D_E10': [['A', 45.0], ['B', 65.0]],
    'E2': [['A', 69.0], ['B', 99.0]],
}
summary = {}
deals = []
for r in rows:
    gid = r['group_id']
    offers = prices.get(gid, [['A', 69.0], ['B', 99.0]])
    deals.append([int(r['customer_id']), offers])
    s = summary.setdefault(gid, {'n': 0, 'seats': 0})
    s['n'] += 1
    s['seats'] += int(r.get('seats') or 0)
print('enterprise actionable', len(rows), summary)
if deals:
    # Chunk defensively though the API supports batch sends.
    ok = fail = 0
    for i in range(0, len(deals), 500):
        res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals[i:i+500])
        results = res.get('results', []) if isinstance(res, dict) else []
        if results:
            ok += sum(1 for x in results if x.get('success'))
            fail += sum(1 for x in results if not x.get('success'))
        else:
            ok += len(deals[i:i+500])
        print(' enterprise chunk', i//500+1, 'result', str(res)[:1000])
    print('enterprise sent ok/fail', ok, fail)
else:
    print('No enterprise deals to send')

verify = nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT et.customer_id, c.group_id, s.status sub_status,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY et.customer_id ORDER BY et.day DESC, et.message_id DESC) rn,
         et.sender, et.closed, et.day
  FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c ON et.customer_id=c.customer_id
  LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=et.customer_id
  WHERE c.customer_type='large'
)
SELECT group_id, sender, day, sub_status, COUNT(*) n
FROM latest WHERE rn=1 AND sender!='agent' AND (closed=0 OR closed IS NULL)
GROUP BY group_id, sender, day, sub_status ORDER BY day DESC
""")['rows']
print('remaining latest non-agent', verify[:50])

# Print current cost info sanity.
print('done')

import novamind_api as nm, json, math
D=nm.vars.current_day
start=D-6
prev=D-13

def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    warn=r.get('warning') or r.get('warnings')
    if warn: print('WARNING', warn)
    return r['rows']

def pp(title, rows, maxn=30):
    print('\n--', title, f'({len(rows)})')
    for row in rows[:maxn]: print(row)
    if len(rows)>maxn: print('...')

print('day',D)
pp('ledger by category last7', q(f"SELECT category, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>={start} AND day<={D} GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC"),80)
pp('ledger by day last21', q(f"SELECT day, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) net, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN amount>0 THEN amount ELSE 0 END),2) income, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN amount<0 THEN amount ELSE 0 END),2) cost FROM ledger WHERE day>={D-20} GROUP BY day ORDER BY day"),30)
pp('subscription payments by day last35', q(f"SELECT day, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>={D-34} AND category='subscription_payment' GROUP BY day ORDER BY day"),50)
pp('cost categories last7', q(f"SELECT category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>={start} AND day<={D} AND amount<0 GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt"),80)
pp('active subs by group plan list promo top', q("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, ROUND(s.listed_price,2) listed, ROUND(s.promotion,2) promo, ROUND(s.effective_price,2) eff,
       COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, ROUND(s.listed_price,2), ROUND(s.promotion,2), ROUND(s.effective_price,2)
ORDER BY mrr DESC
"""),100)
# due next 7 using billing mod: days D+1..D+7? billing_day_mod30 equal day%30 maybe payments occur at start_day + n*30; if current day day value. Use mod of due days.
mods=[(D+i)%30 for i in range(1,8)]
modstr=','.join(map(str,mods))
pp('due next7 active by group/plan/price (mods)', q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, ROUND(s.listed_price,2) listed, ROUND(s.promotion,2) promo, ROUND(s.effective_price,2) eff,
       COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) curr_mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({modstr})
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, ROUND(s.listed_price,2), ROUND(s.promotion,2), ROUND(s.effective_price,2)
ORDER BY curr_mrr DESC
"""),100)
pp('billing mods active all', q("""SELECT s.billing_day_mod30, COUNT(*) subs, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr FROM subscriptions s WHERE status='subscribed' GROUP BY s.billing_day_mod30 ORDER BY s.billing_day_mod30"""),40)
pp('S2 start cohorts active recent', q(f"""
SELECT s.start_day, s.plan, ROUND(s.listed_price,0) listed, ROUND(s.promotion,0) promo, COUNT(*) subs, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),0) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.group_id='S2' AND s.start_day>={D-60}
GROUP BY s.start_day, s.plan, ROUND(s.listed_price,0), ROUND(s.promotion,0)
ORDER BY s.start_day DESC, mrr DESC LIMIT 200
"""),250)
pp('new subs last7 by group/source/plan/list/promo', q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.plan, ROUND(s.listed_price,0) listed, ROUND(s.promotion,0) promo, ROUND(s.effective_price,0) eff, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),0) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.start_day>={start}
GROUP BY c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.plan, ROUND(s.listed_price,0), ROUND(s.promotion,0), ROUND(s.effective_price,0)
ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100
"""),120)
pp('new lead outcomes last7 by group/status/source', q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status, COUNT(*) n
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE c.created_day>={start} AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status
ORDER BY c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status
"""),120)
pp('ad channel leads by week/group', q(f"""
SELECT channel_id, group_id, SUM(leads_generated) leads, ROUND(SUM(spend),2) spend, ROUND(SUM(spend)*1.0/NULLIF(SUM(leads_generated),0),2) cpl
FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day>={start} AND day<={D} GROUP BY channel_id, group_id ORDER BY spend DESC
"""),50)
pp('ad channel leads by day', q(f"""
SELECT day, channel_id, group_id, ROUND(SUM(leads_generated),2) leads, ROUND(SUM(spend),2) spend, ROUND(SUM(spend)*1.0/NULLIF(SUM(leads_generated),0),2) cpl
FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day>={D-13} GROUP BY day, channel_id, group_id ORDER BY day, spend DESC
"""),100)
pp('cancellations last7 by plan/list/promo/start top', q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, ROUND(s.listed_price,0) listed, ROUND(s.promotion,0) promo, s.start_day, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),0) lost_mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>={start}
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, ROUND(s.listed_price,0), ROUND(s.promotion,0), s.start_day
ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 120
"""),150)
pp('downgrades plan_changes last7?', q(f"""
SELECT * FROM ledger WHERE day>={start} AND category LIKE '%downgrade%' LIMIT 20
"""),20)
# maybe plan_change_events table? show tables?
pp('open issues by group age', q("SELECT group_id, days_open, COUNT(*) n FROM issues WHERE status='open' GROUP BY group_id, days_open ORDER BY group_id, days_open"),80)
pp('resolved issues last7 by group lag', q(f"SELECT group_id, days_open, COUNT(*) n FROM issues WHERE status='resolved' AND resolved_day>={start} GROUP BY group_id, days_open ORDER BY group_id, days_open"),80)
pp('service day last14', q(f"SELECT * FROM service_day WHERE day>={D-13} ORDER BY day"),20)
# active issue age for subscribed only
pp('open issues active subscribers by group age', q("""
SELECT i.group_id, i.days_open, COUNT(*) n
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id, i.days_open ORDER BY i.group_id, i.days_open
"""),80)
# enterprise latest summaries
pp('latest non-agent enterprise actionable by group/thread/day/status', q("""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT et.*, c.group_id, s.status sub_status, s.seat_count sub_seats, s.plan sub_plan, s.effective_price sub_eff,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY et.customer_id ORDER BY et.day DESC, et.message_id DESC) rn
  FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c ON et.customer_id=c.customer_id
  LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=et.customer_id
)
SELECT group_id, sender, thread_type, day, closed, close_reason, sub_status, COUNT(*) n, SUM(COALESCE(sub_seats, seat_count)) seats, AVG(turn_number) avg_turn,
       MIN(turn_number) min_turn, MAX(turn_number) max_turn
FROM latest WHERE rn=1 AND sender!='agent' AND (closed=0 OR closed IS NULL)
GROUP BY group_id, sender, thread_type, day, closed, close_reason, sub_status
ORDER BY day DESC, seats DESC
"""),200)
pp('latest agent offers still lead by group/day', q("""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT et.*, c.group_id, s.status sub_status, s.seat_count sub_seats,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY et.customer_id ORDER BY et.day DESC, et.message_id DESC) rn
  FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c ON et.customer_id=c.customer_id
  LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=et.customer_id
)
SELECT group_id, day, sub_status, COUNT(*) n, SUM(COALESCE(sub_seats, seat_count)) seats, MIN(turn_number) min_turn, MAX(turn_number) max_turn
FROM latest WHERE rn=1 AND sender='agent' AND sub_status='lead'
GROUP BY group_id, day, sub_status ORDER BY day DESC, seats DESC
"""),200)
pp('latest closed enterprise by close reason last14', q(f"""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT et.*, c.group_id, s.status sub_status, s.seat_count sub_seats,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY et.customer_id ORDER BY et.day DESC, et.message_id DESC) rn
  FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c ON et.customer_id=c.customer_id
  LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=et.customer_id
)
SELECT group_id, close_reason, sub_status, COUNT(*) n, SUM(COALESCE(sub_seats, seat_count)) seats, MIN(day) min_day, MAX(day) max_day
FROM latest WHERE rn=1 AND closed=1 AND day>={D-21}
GROUP BY group_id, close_reason, sub_status ORDER BY max_day DESC, seats DESC
"""),100)
pp('sample actionable enterprise messages', q("""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT et.*, c.group_id, c.company_primary_concern, c.company_size_descriptor, c.company_decision_style,
         s.status sub_status, s.seat_count sub_seats,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY et.customer_id ORDER BY et.day DESC, et.message_id DESC) rn
  FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c ON et.customer_id=c.customer_id
  LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=et.customer_id
)
SELECT customer_id, group_id, day, turn_number, thread_type, seat_count AS msg_seats, sub_seats, company_size_descriptor, company_primary_concern, company_decision_style,
       substr(message_text,1,350) msg, offer_json
FROM latest WHERE rn=1 AND sender!='agent' AND (closed=0 OR closed IS NULL)
ORDER BY day DESC, group_id, COALESCE(sub_seats, seat_count) DESC LIMIT 80
"""),100)
